selectedSlice: Open the full submission timeline inline from student requirement detail.
selectionReason: Current repo evidence showed a real same-panel follow-through gap, not speculative polish. Requirement detail already showed summary facts, latest feedback, and matching uploaded work, but students still had to leave that panel and open Feedback History or Submitted Work to inspect the full submission timeline. Reusing the existing student-safe `/api/reviews/:submissionId/history` route and the current in-page timeline renderer kept the slice small, exact, and route-free while staying inside the same requirement panel.
userFacingImprovement: Students can now open the full submission timeline from requirement detail and review versions, status changes, and visible teacher notes without leaving the same panel.
privacyAndPermissionNotes: The slice changes only the signed-in student dashboard rendering and reuses the current student-safe review-history route already enforced by existing auth checks.
No auth, RBAC, tenant, site, program, mentor-assignment, or student-privacy boundary changed.
No new route, payload expansion, or staff-only comment exposure was introduced.
Requirement detail timeline state now clears when the selected requirement is hidden so stale timeline context does not linger behind another phase filter.

validation.notes: Focused workspace coverage passed before docs/state closeout, and the broader validation ladder passed after the closeout files were updated. git diff --check reported only CRLF normalization warnings.

deferred: Filtered Audit cross-school proof still needs a tighter locally provable scenario before another audit-focused pass.
Student next-step anchor follow-through is broader than this requirement-detail timeline batch.
Student evidence-panel language cleanup remains lower value than the selected same-panel handoff.
Broader Global Admin route refresh remains larger than one safe hourly slice.
Site selector follow-through still needs a broader cross-surface contract.
Mentor reassignment and removal workflow still needs mutation and audit design.
Administration hosted read-only click-through refresh still needs allowed runtime.
Hosted section-level permission proof still needs allowed runtime and audit-event writes.
nextRecommended: Continue the student refresh only if the current requirement detail or next-step list can prove another exact same-panel handoff after the new inline timeline; otherwise return to filtered Audit cross-school proof only if a tighter local scenario is now provable.
doNotRepeat: Do not remove the inline requirement-detail submission timeline while the current student dashboard and tests already prove the same-panel handoff through the existing review-history route.
Do not regress requirement detail back to summary-only latest feedback while the current same-panel timeline already proves the fuller submission context.
Do not expose staff-only comments, new evidence routes, or other-student submission context while deepening student requirement detail.
